name: Alison
address: 110 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10009, USA

reviews.author: Regina Foster
reviews.text: Really enjoyed our meal at Alison at St. Mark’s. The crispy calamari was great, and the onion soup was warm and comforting especially when it’s so cold outside! We also tried the pasta and the salmon burger — both were very good. For dessert, we had the Belgian waffle to my taste was a bit too sweet and New York cheesecake was great. Nice atmosphere and solid food overall. Would definitely come back.

reviews.author: Hannah Tam-Claiborne
reviews.text: Came to this cute little eatery with some friends over the weekend. The atmosphere was warm and welcoming, from the lighting, decor and thoughtfully curated playlist, the vibes were immaculate. Vincent our server was so attentive and knowledgeable about the wine selection and gave us a great recommendation, we loved the rioja. We also tried the cocktails and the hibiscus mule was lovely. I will definitely be coming back and introducing my other friends to this amazing spot!

reviews.author: Dean J
reviews.text: I popped into this places for happy hour because it had two of my favorite things: wings and oysters! They were both good and fresh. I think I’ll certainly come back to try some of the other things.

name: JOJU - East Village
address: 33 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: David Castillo
reviews.text:
Just came here to get a quick bite to eat and I was shocked how big thee sandwiches were. I got the caramelized pork sandwich and it was amazing, super flavorful, spicy but hurts good, and very filling.

I’m definitely gonna come back here and try their wings and other food.

reviews.author: Michael Krynski
reviews.text:
It’s impossible to make a good Banh Mi sandwich if your bread is stale !!
The carrots were not properly marinated , one more little detail for this classic sandwich use cilantro, not parsley😵‍💫 bottom line amateur hour stay away

reviews.author: Z yuu
reviews.text: The service was quick and the people were so kind and sweet!

name: KENKA
address: 25 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Adam Zdeb
reviews.text: Amazing authentic Japanese eatery, all the food was cheap and tasty, our favorites were the pork belly yakitori and tonkatsu kimchi. We did try some delicious home made Japanese sodas peach was our favorite. Once we got the bill the waiter brought us 2 mini cups with flavored sugar. We did not what it was for so he told us there is a cotton candy machine outside where everyone can make their own desert. That was a cool touch. Best time to come is when they are opening at 5 if you don't want to wait. If you come later be prepared because this place gets packed.

reviews.author: 702 Web Marketing
reviews.text:
Great spot for a fun night out with friends. The atmosphere is lively, energetic, and definitely on the louder side, which adds to the experience if you’re looking for a vibrant social vibe rather than a quiet dinner. One fun touch I appreciated was the cotton candy machine near the front while waiting, it adds a playful and memorable element.

The food felt genuinely authentic, with a solid Japanese menu and a nice bonus of having a separate late night menu after 8 PM, which gives you more options depending on when you go. Everything we tried was flavorful, satisfying, and surprisingly well priced for the quality, making it a great value overall.

A couple things to note, if you’re on the larger side, the chairs may feel a bit small or less comfortable. Also, service can slow down when they get busy, so it helps to go in with a little patience during peak hours.

Overall, a really enjoyable experience with great food, fun energy, and strong value for the money. I’d definitely recommend it for a casual group hangout or late night bite.

reviews.author: Skyler Donner
reviews.text:
This place is the definition of “don’t judge a book by its cover.” From the outside, it’s very under assuming, but once you step in and try the food, it absolutely over delivers in every way.

We ordered a wide variety of dishes, and honestly, everything that hit the table was incredible. The flavors were bold, fresh, and clearly made with care. The standout for me was the beef stir fry—perfectly cooked, tender, and packed with flavor. The chicken kabobs were just as impressive, juicy and seasoned to perfection with that ideal balance of char and tenderness.

The atmosphere is… definitely something. It’s loud, energetic, and a bit chaotic—probably not for everyone—but it gives the place a unique personality that you won’t forget. It’s the kind of spot that grabs your attention and keeps things interesting from start to finish.

Fair warning—the line is almost always out the door, which tells you everything you need to know. Definitely come early or be prepared to wait, but trust me, it’s worth it.

If you’re willing to look past the unassuming exterior and embrace the wild vibe inside, you’re in for an amazing meal. I’ll definitely be back.

name: Cafe Mogador
address: 101 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10009, USA

reviews.author: Selin Ozguler
reviews.text:
I had the Eggs Benedict and it was absolutely delicious. My friend ordered a halloumi dish, which was also very good. The mimosa was excellent as well, and the prices were reasonable for the quality of the food and overall experience.
The only issue was that although we had a reservation for three people, we were seated at a table for two because our third guest had not arrived yet. I don’t think that was the right approach and it felt a bit disappointing.
Other than that, everything was great. The food, drinks, and atmosphere were all enjoyable. I would definitely come back.

reviews.author: kilam
reviews.text:
Food was amazing! We also got eggs Benedict? Unsure of the name exactly. I was distracted and didn’t take a picture. All was really good and service was fast & friendly.

I have to say. I cannot stress this enough, BEST FALAFEL I have ever had. I turned a falafel skeptic into a falafel lover. I told him if falafel tastes bad it’s just old or masses produced. This felt like the real thing!!

reviews.author: Mrs Heapy
reviews.text: Food definitively the highlight of this location. Food prepared with balance and care. Hot sauce with hummus was amazing and eggs very well prepared. Salad with beets and vinegrette excellent. The postcard with the image of the owners grandparents made the visit more meaningful. Busy but service prompt and seating quaint. Will visit again.

name: Pizza Studio Tamaki
address: 123 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10009, USA

reviews.author: Cliff Jones
reviews.text: The dough is so soft on the inside yet crispy enough on the outside. Recommend trying a few different pizzas. Nice drink menu as well. Tried the arancini as well but forgot to take a photo--it was light on the inside and fried the Japanese way, not too fried, really good. Try the 5 formaggi with added prosciutto for light saltiness from the meat and underlying flavors of the cheeses. Tamaki was nice, Bismarck with the runny egg was excellent. Dessert was refreshing. A bit in the more expensive side because we tried a little bit of everything but certainly worth it. Definitely recommend making a reservation ahead of time if possible. My friend and I walked up 10 mins before opening and were 2nd in line behind one person and we were put on the waitlist expecting to be seated within 2.5 hours. We checked back an hour later and they had a couple bar seats for us I believe because an earlier reservation had left already. Excited to try an original PST restaurant next time I'm in Japan.

reviews.author: Min Kim
reviews.text: The best pizza I’ve ever had! It’s a very thin, light pizza, but none of my slices were soggy throughout. It’s an amazing and delicious pizza. 🥰

reviews.author: Kjerstie O
reviews.text: Really enjoyed the pizzas!! Apps and dessert were not worth it imo. Service was struggling but I’ll write it off as getting things sorted for their first week of opening

name: Crispiano
address: 130 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10009, USA

reviews.author: TomFun
reviews.text: Fried calamari was so delicious, hot and crispy. Good pizza. However, the carbonara spaghetti we had was just weird. There was a gummy chunk of Parmesan cheese and slightly slimy egg sauce on the pasta. I wish they shaved the cheese and mixed with the egg sauce as what you expect for carbonara. Weird twist  of the traditional menu and I didn't like it. Was it simply a mistake??

reviews.author: Bob Burger
reviews.text: Very very very mediocre. We got the caeser salad which was really whatever. Its seems like they got pre-chopped bag lettuce with Sysco croutons and dressing and threw it together. We also got pizza which was also very whatever. The cheese tasted and felt almost gummy. The pizza lacked any salt, pepper, or any other seasoning. The lack of seasoning seemed to common theme. We also got bolognese which was watery and flavorless.

reviews.author: Penny
reviews.text: absolutely fantastic happy hour deals. bottomless brunch Saturday and Sunday. food was also good and fair priced. would come back for a dinner or lunch to try their other dishes. also, waiter was a G.

name: Nan Xiang Soup Dumplings - East Village
address: 15 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: California Calamansi
reviews.text:
Delicious dumplings!

We stopped by on a Monday evening and only had about a 5-minute wait. They use a convenient QR code waitlist, which made things easy.

We ordered the vegetable, crab, and signature pork soup dumplings. This was my first time seeing vegetable soup dumplings, and I was intrigued. The filling was packed with fresh green vegetables, though I didn’t find much broth inside. The pork and crab dumplings, however, had the perfect balance of flavorful broth and filling. The wrappers were tender and delicate while still holding together beautifully.

Another standout was the bok choy—fresh, vibrant, and incredibly tasty.

We also ordered the vegetable fried noodles, which were a huge hit. The vegetables were plentiful and perfectly cooked, and the sauce tied everything together wonderfully. I could honestly eat this dish every day!

A great meal overall, and I’d definitely come back for more. ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

reviews.author: J P
reviews.text:
This was our first time visiting this place, and we will definitely be back to try more dumplings. The soup dumplings are awesome! We had the Crab Meat & Pork Soup Dumplings and the Truffle & Pork Soup Dumplings — the flavor and taste were amazing. Shanghai Siu Mai - It wasn't bad, but just not our favorite. It was missing flavor.
Just be aware that the Beef Tripe in Spicy Sauce appetizer is served cold, and the sauce for the Pan-Fried Crispy Noodles was not very tasty. The beef itself was delicious, but the sauce tasted a bit bland.
The staff was very helpful and kind.

reviews.author: Griana
reviews.text:
I came here for a Christmas gathering with work, and the scallion pancakes convinced me I had to bring my wife back. It did not disappoint. She got the pork and cabbage wonton soup and the crispy shrimp, which she loved. I got the vegetarian soup dumplings: perfect for an inter-vegetarian/meat-eater relationship. My wife left saying she hadn’t felt so satiated in a long time. We also got the grapefruit soju, which was light, sweet, and delicious.

Our waiter was great, genuinely friendly and provided excellent customer service.

name: Loong Noodles
address: 28 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Jimmy Zhao
reviews.text:
Really enjoyed my meal at Loong Noodles! The food was delicious, affordable, and comforting.

The lamb rice noodle soup combo was a great deal for around $20 and even came with a side dish and drink. The broth was light and flavorful, not overly oily or spicy, which made it feel healthier than a lot of other noodle spots.

The beef noodle soup had tender beef and a rich broth, and the secret pork sauce noodles were super tasty and full of flavor. Also loved the crispy pork pan-fried dumplings and refreshing cucumber salad.

reviews.author: Jennifer Lee
reviews.text: Great place for quick and valued noodles. Noodles are around $12 and they give generous portions! Super yummy. The spicy chili wontons and were def my fav. The noodles were a perfect texture and super flavorful. Would come back.

reviews.author: Artem Bakatov
reviews.text:
Yum. Good combo for $20 - noodles side and a drink. Obviously a coke in my situation.

The texture of the noodle is perfect, chewy and holds the spice beautifully. I got medium spice to test out the waters but will get extra spice next time as I had to add some chili oil to it.  The cucumber salad was perfectly seasoned and a good addition to spicy noodles.

Service is quick noodles came 3 mins after ordering. However, regarding personnelle there is nothing special - they just do their jobs.

name: Soothr
address: 204 E 13th St, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Oak Jerin
reviews.text:
Soothr is hands down one of my favorite Thai restaurants in NYC. The food is consistently delicious, the atmosphere is lively and welcoming, and every dish we ordered was packed with flavor.
What really made our experience exceptional was the service. A huge thank you to TK, PP, Popeye and Kyle, who went above and beyond to make us feel welcome. They were attentive, friendly, knowledgeable, and genuinely cared about making sure we had a great dining experience. Outstanding hospitality like this is hard to find and truly sets Soothr apart.
If you’re looking for amazing Thai food paired with top-notch service, Soothr is a must-visit. We’ll definitely be back!

reviews.author: Davina Srioudom
reviews.text: Amazing food!! Service was so good and quick. We walked in without a reservation and seated immediately (party of 3). Yaya was our waitress and was very attentive - quick to take our order and consistently refilling our water without asking. Super kind and checked in about allergies as well. Food was yummy, overall a great experience!

reviews.author: Jonathan K
reviews.text: We visited after watching the Bon Appétit video on YouTube and hearing that Soothr is a Michelin Guide restaurant, and it absolutely lived up to the acclaim. Everything we ordered was thoughtfully prepared, full of flavor, and clearly deserving of all the praise. My favorites were the Khao Soi with Fried Prawn and the Pad Thai — some of the best Thai food you’ll eat. I would definitely come back the next time I’m in NYC.

name: Spot Dessert Bar East Village
address: 13 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Rachel Braddock
reviews.text:
This place is incredible! They’re highly efficient without ever compromising on service or quality.

When we first arrived, there was an understandable one-hour wait, but they kept us updated via text and notified us as soon as our table was ready.

The menu included pictures of every item, which made ordering easy and convenient!

We chose the Milky Puff and Sesame Bloom, and both were outstanding with truly unique flavors.

If you’re not into overly sweet desserts but still want something indulgent, the Sesame Bloom is perfect for you. If you prefer something fun and nostalgic, try the Milky Puff — it’s like enjoying cereal and ice cream together, but in the best possible way lol!

reviews.author: Lyds R.
reviews.text: Spot Dessert Bar has been on my list for a while and is a relatively small space down a staircase, don’t miss it! We went in a Saturday afternoon so it was pretty crowded, but were able to be seated immediately! The menu is focused on a few key dessert items, and there are many different combinations you can make. We got the matcha lava cake to share between 2 people, which I’d recommend since it can get heavy. The lava cake itself was so decadent, the dark chocolate was super rich and it was so satisfying to see the melting chocolate ooze out! It tastes really wonderful with the matcha ice cream, and is certainly a great flavor profile. I’d definitely recommend for dessert lovers!

reviews.author: Katie Lee
reviews.text:
Spot is my favorite dessert place!

They consistently serve delicious desserts, and the variety is impressive. Everything I’ve tried has been great so far. The items that come with the ice cream are especially good — the combinations are always creative and flavorful.

Every time I visit, choosing what to order is part of the fun because there are so many tempting options. The coffee drinks are excellent as well and pair perfectly with the desserts.

It’s also a great spot for a weekend evening date.
Highly recommended.

name: Café Maud
address: 132 2nd Ave, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Paul Vetrano
reviews.text: We cane as a family after my daughter's graduation. The Smash Burger, fries and frozen Aperol Spritz were worth the visit.  They were all delicious and tasted literally out of this world! Very friendly hostess and personable enough waitress. Pizza was a bit too doughy...however, everyone ended their meals. We all agreed that we would return!

reviews.author: Joy
reviews.text: Literally a wonderful brunch place! Their food is really good, and fresh and whole. We got a fresh juice, and an amazing Chai. They are very accommodating and understanding and kind staff and facility. We also had an Irish waiter! I hope he still works there! Very friendly!

reviews.author: Emma Greenberg
reviews.text:
Went to Cafe Maud’s coffee window and my friend and I ordered a Maple Latte and a Strawberry Matcha. Both were delicious, my friend said he was “very impressed” with his maple latte. The matcha had oat milk, per my request, and it was still creamy and frothy… and the strawberry flavor was not too sweet!

We went at a time when there was no line, and the service was very polite, efficient, and friendly. Was nice they brought out the drinks without the lids so we could see their beautiful latte art, but also provided lids on the side without us needing to ask. Will definitely be back!

name: CHELI MANHATTAN 浙里
address: 19 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Kate Zuckerman
reviews.text: We had an amazing dinner here at Che li! The service was so great and attentive. The food came out perfect timing and everything was delicious. The soup dumplings were the best and the portion were great for sharing. We left so full and they even gave us a complimentary dessert on the house which was warm and delicious. I’ll definitely be coming back here for a date night or girls night out. The vibe is so cute and the drinks are also amazing! The tea is perfect for a cold winter night.

reviews.author: Daniel Jeng
reviews.text:
Had a great meal at Cheli, really solid spot overall.

Crispy Smoked Fish: 5/5, perfectly fried with a nice crunch and a great sweet and sour sauce. Just watch out for small bones.

Sweet and Sour Eel: 3.5/5, tender and flavorful but a bit too oily.

Longjin Shrimp: 5/5, best dish of the night. Super tender, melt in your mouth with a great vinegar pairing.

Lotus Pond Stir Fry: 4/5, light, fresh, and flavorful. Would recommend.

West Lake Minced Beef Soup: 3.5/5, slightly darker and richer than expected but still decent.

Hot Stone Vegetable and Pork Rice: 4.5/5, crispy rice with plenty of toppings. Great balance to the meal.

Jasmine Tea Pudding: 4/5, strong floral flavor at first but grew on me.

Overall, really enjoyed it and would come back.

reviews.author: Sa Sa su
reviews.text:
The interior is unique and adds a lot of character to the restaurant, creating a fun and memorable atmosphere. The servers were friendly, and most of them speak Mandarin, which made communication easy.

The food was good overall, though not particularly outstanding. One highlight was the fried pumpkin with salted egg yolk—perfectly balanced between sweet and savory, and definitely a must-try. The crab minced tofu, however, lacked a strong crab flavor, which was a bit disappointing. The house fish stew with mustard greens leaned a little too sour for my taste.

They also have some fun and creative presentations, including dishes with fire elements, which adds to the experience. The chicken wrapped in leaves was a bit overhyped and smaller than expected, although the meat itself was tender and well-cooked.

Overall, a solid dining experience with a great ambiance, but the flavors didn’t quite reach the same level as the presentation.

name: Empellon Al Pastor
address: 132 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10009, USA

reviews.author: Elisabeth Ray
reviews.text:
A bit crowded & noisy for my tastes BUT, manageable. EXCELLENT food and strong drinks!!! Immaculate bathroom that's handicap accessible.
Crowd looks like NYU trust fund kids and trendy hipsters but, I enjoyed this spot immensely and WILL BE BACK!! A gem of deliciously delightful Mexican food in the East Village!!

reviews.author: Blake Rasmussen
reviews.text:
Edited April 2026 - added photos of Gordo More-Ita Munch special and the bacon-wrapped hot dog. The gordo was not particularly good value at $12 and I wasn’t super impressed with - unmelted cheese, not super full, and about 3 times more expensive than its Taco Bell equivalent (shout out to Taco Bell for value). The bacon wrapped hot dog was better, and though it’s $9 I would get it again!

A great little dive bar experience with great Mexican food. The food menu is great, with a few non standard items like a sandwich special (which was meatloaf when we went), and the tacos look great though are very expensive at $7 each. The nachos were incredible value, and every ingredient was handmade down to the chips. They offer a happy hour special on drinks, margaritas can be customized with either tequila or mezqual, and the beer & shot combo is a great deal at $10. They also have a shot that contains mezcal and cold brew for people drinking all night who need a pick me up.

reviews.author: Issei Kohama
reviews.text: Great atmosphere for hanging out but the food is most definately overhyped and the bartender wasn’t friendly. Did not meet my expectation given all the hype on social media.

name: Mokyo
address: 109 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10009, USA

reviews.author: Rachel Braddock
reviews.text:
Chefs at Mokyo know what they are doing. I never had Korean tapas before but I am a huge fan now.

While I am not Korean, I usually hold Korean food to a high standard because I grew up in a Korean neighborhood and many of my friends were Korean, so I was constantly surrounded by — and eating — authentic cuisine. While this spot isn’t traditional, it’s absolutely worth trying. The food and service are both incredible.

This place is super impressive and makes for a great experience, especially for a date night or a dinner with a friend. I wouldn’t recommend it for parties larger than two, though, since the dish portions are on the smaller side.

We started with the yuzu strawberry and golden punch mocktails, which were served in adult Capri Sun-style pouches — such a fun touch! For dinner, we had the corn dumplings, oxtail spring rolls, noodles, scallops, and ragu rice. My only critique is that the spring rolls could use a bit of oil blotting before serving, so they don’t taste too much like the fryer. Other than that, every dish was delicious. The textures and sauces were spot-on.

I’ll probably be thinking about the tapioca rice balls in the ragu and the mushrooms from the scallop dish for the rest of the week — seriously, so scrumptious!

10/10 recommend Mokyo!

reviews.author: Guy Eichler
reviews.text:
Mokyo is a very unique place. Every dish is a unique invention and all of it just tastes good. I haven’t had something I didn’t like and all dishes are significantly different from each other.

This is the sister restaurant to Thursday Kitchen and the concept is pretty similar. Innovative dishes that represent a fusion between Korean food to everything else in the world. The main difference between Mokyo and Thursday kitchen is that Mokyo is the upscale version and you need a reservation, it is not a walk-in place.

Notice that portions are pretty small and the prices are slightly expensive for the portion. It’s not going to be a cheap meal if you’re hungry.

My favorite was the scallops dish which was really hard to stop eating. If I had room for an extra dish I’d had a second scallop. The gnocchi special was also very good and might be the best value for money. Lobster roll was the least value for money.

Overall highly recommended.

reviews.author: Chutao Zeng
reviews.text: As an avid Korean food enjoyer blessed with easy access to Northern Blvd, it is to my oversight that I came with the expectation for Korean food tonight. Instead, I got truffle oil gnocchi (best dish of the night), chili with rice cake (3piece of rice cakes), bbq ribs (ribs did not fall off the bone), and a clam chowder with scallop that looks pan seared by actually just chewy. It’s not you, it’s me. I’ll try to book Moono earlier or stick to outerboro Korean food.

name: The Rhymers' Club
address: 37 1/2 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Steve Martin
reviews.text: Efrain, Jordi, and Artemio are the best barbacks in the entire world, they always have the bar set up and stocked and ready for a fun night at work. They stock the glassware very nicely and have never in their life broken a glass. 10/10 would come back just to see them refill juices with the best precision .

reviews.author: Jaye Aster Broder
reviews.text: This is a large space and suitable for event are large groups. The cocktail selection is quite expansive with a lot of variety. Staff are very friendly. The bathrooms require you go down stairs. I tried three bourbon based cocktails: the poet’s prescription, remember me, and midnight sky. The midnight sky tasted a little like vegetables which isn’t really my preference. The remember me was very smooth. The pizza and dumplings are good although the pizza edges were burned sometimes.

reviews.author: Maisie-Kate Keane
reviews.text: I recently hosted my birthday at The Rhymers Club, and it was hands down the best night ever! From start to finish, everything was perfect. The drinks were incredible, the staff were so friendly and attentive - they felt like part of the party, and the DJ kept us dancing all night long. The interiors are absolutely stunning, a great date spot!  Special shout-out to Jack, who was so kind to reserve a space for me and my friends for the evening. Everyone had an amazing time, and I can’t wait to go back! Highly recommend this spot for any celebration or night out! 🤍

name: Hanoi House
address: 119 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10009, USA

reviews.author: Sarah
reviews.text:
Friend and I dined in during happy hour. We got in without reservations on Friday night. The happy hour specials had the cocktails discounted more steeply than any of the food items. Place was not too busy and there were lots of kids around. It’s a pretty narrow space though so I wouldn’t say it is very accessible. We had to take our coats off the coat hooks so that they didn’t bother the group sitting next to us.

The portions are on the smaller side and I saw that group sitting next to us just ordered an entire second order of pho.

The food was amazing though and I was tempted to do that myself. I had never had pho with the condiments like pickled garlic, this particular chili sauce which I ordered seconds of, and a ginger condiment which was also great.

That being said I don’t think it really needed any more flavor as the veggie broth was so aromatic. One of my favorite vegetarian phos ever. Would just prefer more tofu.

Food came out super fast and everything was cooked well.

reviews.author: Najeeb Abdullah
reviews.text: Cozy Vietnamese cusine on the lower east side. We stopped by for lunch and it was quite busy. Luckily we had placed a reservation so we didnt have to wait long. The restaurant has a nice ambiance, perfect blend of casual and intimate. We ordered the spicy garlic cauliflower as an appetizer, and for main dish we got vegetarian Banh Mi and Pho. The cauliflower was very good, the spicy seasoning and oil was very flavorful. The Banh Mi was crunchy and delicious, the fried tofu was well seasoned. The Pho broth definitely hit the spot on a cold day. Service was great and pricing was reasonable and fair.

reviews.author: Travel Eat
reviews.text: A treat of a place on St.Mark’s. Upon arriving, you walk in to the bar area, greeted by a smiling face who takes you down a few stairs into a charming dining area. The smell of the food is what grabs you first, and then you look down at the plates on the tables as you walk by them and see the enormous servings. Your hunger goes crazy and it’s almost torture, knowing that you’re going have to wait to eat some of this amazing food. They bring you to your table in a small nook and, within seconds, you are greeted by your server. With enthusiasm and detail, they describe the dishes. I went with the chicken pho. It was served within minutes and it was fantastic! Great portion size cooked to perfection! Hunger fulfilled, I left a fat and happy onto my day in the Lower East Side. I will be returning often. If you haven’t done so already, give this place a try. You will love it!

address: 26 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Kevin Hsieh
reviews.text:
If you're in need of a taiwanese food fix, this would be a great spot to scratch that itch.

The crispy silken tofu was amazing, with that cumin lime and chili oil vinaigrette- awesome appetizer to get started with.
The scallion pancake beef wrap came with a very generous portion of that tender and flavorful beef shank, and was well balanced with the onions and pickled vegetables.
Both the dry beef noodle and the beef noodle soup had bouncy noodles that really soaked up all the flavor. Only complaint would be that the soup is quite salty, but at least still had all the right spices and flavors.

Overall, 886 seems to be a trendy, boisterous place to get some solid taiwanese flavors, and to hang out and grab drinks with friends.

reviews.author: Jack Tuggle
reviews.text:
Taiwanese food is always a good choice however I feel like New York City doesn’t quite embrace how amazing the food is this place is cute. Small and quaint. Staff is super friendly and overall we had a good time.

Food – the food here is all good. The portions were nice and overall we had a good meal. My friend was the one who recommended this place because he mentioned the sandwich here is supposed to be one of the best in the city and I love fried chicken sandwiches.

Overall, we have the fried rice where we added sausage the fried chicken sandwich, an extra order of french fries with the Taiwan dust and everything was good

My biggest complaint is that the sandwich is so big and honestly a little too messy for me. They put pickles on the bottom, which makes everything slide off and not the best way to build a sandwich the flavor however it was delicious. The sauce is delicious. The fries were amazing the Taiwan dust and seaweed.

Ambience - the place is very small and I think only one other person eating there so not very busy very quiet. It almost felt very often affected Taiwan. They also had a banging playlist metal tables and chairs with no backs. Simple.

reviews.author: David Joseph
reviews.text:
We came to this restaurant on a very cold New Year’s Day for lunch. We made a reservation for two a few hours before on Open Table. When we came to the restaurant there were several people but there appeared to be availability for walk-ins.

While there was one server for the restaurant, she was very polite and attentive. The space is very casual but clean. Despite it being very cold outside, the restaurant was a comfortable temperature. The restaurant is also small but doesn’t feel cramped.

I haven’t had a lot of Taiwanese cuisine but I enjoyed this meal. I thought the Taiwanese sausage was incredible. The portions were also decent size.

I would go back and I would recommend.

name: Baohaus
address: 97 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10009, USA

reviews.author: Isaiah Carter
reviews.text:
The drinks were flowing on a nice hot Saturday. Years ago, I stumbled into Baohaus on 14th street and made it my home.

The chairman bao and the taro fries were good substitutes for me vs. the standard hamburger and fries. I was sad to discover one day that Eddie decided to close that location.

Years later, I’m happy to see the Baohaus has returned to NYC with an expanded menu and new location.

Ordering numerous drinks with a friend today our only concern was the dryness on the Chairman bao.

Happy to see you back Eddie and the whole staff.

reviews.author: Shoooyi
reviews.text:
A place with unique, delicious baos!
I enjoyed the birdhaus baos (which had crispy fried chicken with aioli) and the chairman baos (which had a nutty, sweet pork belly slices). Wasn’t the biggest fan of the Taiwanese wagyu brisket noodle soup, though. I also think it’s a bit pricey.

reviews.author: Polly Anywhere
reviews.text: I love this Sunday afternoon late  lunch / early dinner outdoor in the sun.  The food is super tasty and the service was super wonderful.   The vibe is just perfect in East Village.  yum yum yum

name: Little Kirin
address: 81 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Eden Lin
reviews.text: This was one of the sandwiches of my life. I had the Pho sandwich, Grandma’s braised pork sandwich, and the loaded tater tots. I highly recommend them all. The bread perfectly absorbs the pho broth and it doesn’t disintegrate. Shoutout to Brendan for being a cool guy who created one of the best sandwiches I’ve ever had.

reviews.author: Ray Z
reviews.text: They forgot pho broth and the panko salmon on my tater tots. $10 out of pocket. Only found this out after walking one mile back home.  Make sure you open your bag and each container. This is my third visit but now my last.  Not going back. Couldn’t get any one to pick up the phone. Voice mail not set up

reviews.author: Ruby Chan
reviews.text:
How is lap cheong egg & cheese NOT already a thing everywhere? The Hong Kong girl in me was SCREAMING after my first bite!!!

＊Lap Cheong Egg & Cheese $10
They’re using Chinese sausages from Quang Tran and they do NOT skimp. Literally lap cheong in every bite, and slightly charred from the grill.

The brioche bun was SO soft, and the ginger scallion aioli + melty cheese + runny egg yolk just melted into it. Everything together was actually insane and I can’t go back to a regular BEC anymore lol. I already know I need to go back and try more from Little Kirin.

This limited-time porporeatsgood collab runs all month in May, and $1 from every sandwich goes to Welcome to Chinatown to support the community :)

name: Kimura
address: 31 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Lara
reviews.text: We had a lovely lunch here - perfect for a rainy day! Everything was delicious but their pork belly hot pot, takoyaki and Mentai Mushroom Udon were all highlights! Their Hokkaido cheesecake and handmade Japanese dango are must-try’s as well!

reviews.author: Alexandra Ng
reviews.text: The best place when you’re craving Japanese comfort food and hot pot!! The udon was the perfect texture. The server cooks everything for you and serves your first bowl. It’s super cozy vibes

reviews.author: Eliana Glatt
reviews.text: My friend and I tried the full tasting menu. It was delicious. I highly recommend it, I think the price point is worth it for the amount of food that you get and the quality of the food. The hot pot I would recommend one of the shabu shabu regular flavors (we did the sukiyaki and it was so good). We tried the spicy hiyayakko (tofu in a sauce it was not that exciting) a mix of delicious skewers (the chicken thighs were great), the Kimura roll and both desserts were great.

name: Dumpling N’ Dips
address: 5 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Jaein Kim
reviews.text:
This is the first time I've ever written a review for a restaurant, but I felt like I had to!

The food is delicious. My husband and I got the Thai Lover Set to try all the Thai flavors, and each one is amazing. They are a really fun way to incorporate popular Thai dishes into dumpling form. We even ordered sets of the crab curry, pork basil, chicken green curry, and tom yum dumplings to go so we could have them again the next day!

We also tried the kimchi fries and the shrimp sticks. As a side note, the shrimp sticks are whole shrimps (with cheese!) so they are much plumper than the picture on the menu! The kimchi fries have a slightly sweet and creamy spicy mayo sauce on top which goes really well with the sourness of the kimchi as well.

Last but not least, the service is wonderful! I'm not sure if the person helping us that day is the owner or another staff member, but regardless she is so sweet and kind. We will definitely be visiting Dumplings N' Dips again anytime we're in the city!

reviews.author: Charli Virginia
reviews.text:
What a cute find in the heart of the Village. I quickly ordered shrimp dumplings to try. The woman who helped was very kind. The menu was great, and I wish I had time to try more.

The dumplings, however tasty, appeared more like a steamed shrimp tempura rather than an actual dumpling with shrimp inside. It was good, just not what I was expecting. I would come back to try other options.

reviews.author: Akshay Bhagat
reviews.text:
Budget spot in NYC for unusual but delicious dumplings. Each dumpling has an entire entree inside it so you get to taste 4-5 different "dishes".

Got the thai lover set and included pad thai, massaman curry, pad krapow and green curry, etc.

The dumpling fillings were delicious. The dumplings were also very well made with thin skins.

Service was friendly.

name: Udon St Marks
address: 11 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Gloria Zhang
reviews.text: Pretty good udon and price can't be beat in nyc! Went around 8pm on a Friday night and had to wait about 30 min or so to get in. Got the kitsune and niku udon - both came out piping hot. They're very generous with the niku but wish they gave more tofu pieces with the kitsune udon. Still a pretty good deal and would come again

reviews.author: Elisha Liang
reviews.text: Cute small Japanese cuisine spot, perfect for solo or friends dates. The food is delicious, simple and comforting. Prices are reasonable, one down side is place is small, and cash only 🥲 need to have cash before going. Overall I will go back to try more dishes

reviews.author: jacob goldshtein
reviews.text: Place speaks for itself, I mean just look at the photos. Some of the best udon I’ve had in my life including salaryman udon in suburbs of Tokyo. Small wait outside which was worth it. Cash only but they have an ATM. Service was quick and friendly and the prices were great. If I lived in the are I’d be here at least once a week minimum. Absolutely superb.

name: Thursday Kitchen | Korean
address: 424 E 9th St, New York, NY 10009, USA

reviews.author: Nicole Bonocore
reviews.text:
This is my absolute favorite restaurant in the entire world! I always try to explore new restaurants when i’m in the city, but this is a multiple repeat exception. The food is insanely delicious & consistent every single time. I’ve tried just about everything on the menu, there is not ONE thing that isn’t good! The atmosphere is cozy and the hospitality is always outstanding! Cristal was so so lovely, & the rest of the staff is always extremely attentive & hospitable as well :)

I find myself CRAVING the edamame dumplings! I highly highly recommended giving them a visit!! Seriously love this place so much, they deserve all the love!

reviews.author: Riya Goel
reviews.text: I was so looking forward to coming here! The music is amazing and your water will be filled always. The drinks are great and I think overall a good pregame spot if the wait weren’t so long. The edamame dumplings are definitely worth calling home about but I wouldn’t say much else. The truffle Mac and the gnocchi really left something to be desired, they were either too sweet or heavy as dishes and there was very little balance to be found.

reviews.author: Jonathan Martinez
reviews.text:
Decided to go to Thursday Kitchen on a random and cold monday and it was a fantastic meal. Totally love sharing small plates and love that nothing on the menu surpassed $17.

They serve water as fast as you sit down and have an awesome allergy sheet to alert the kitchen and other staff about what allergies you have.

All the food was pretty awesome I must say.
This is what I ate and what I really loved I put stars next to.

Popcorn Kitchen ***
Plantain ****
Asparagus
Tuna Taco ****
Long Noodle ****
Truffle Mac & Cheese ****
Edamame Dumplings *****
Duck Confit Tacos

Also finished with both desserts (I prefer the Popcorn Ice cream) and their draft beer with yuzu mix. The ginger turmeric tea was also great. Simple and to the point.

I will definitely be back. Also if you’re a blackbird user they take the currensy as well. If you don’t know what that is and want to know hit me up!

name: Anytime Street
address: 34 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Sammi Chan
reviews.text:
A group of my friends and I were visiting NYC and stopped by St. Marks. We needed a quick and affordable fix so we decided to try Anytime Street. It was a no frills, fast and casual eatery.

We’ve never tried a cupbop and was eager to try. Adding dumplings and one of their refreshing drinks was the perfect trio.

Since we had 7 people, we basically ordered everything off the menu and it was delicious!

reviews.author: CAMI FILMS
reviews.text: This place was reasonably priced and very good! The spicy pork and bulgogi. It was flavorful and delicious. I was here with a couple friends and I would absolutely come back.

reviews.author: yolaine rosa
reviews.text: I love the food. Come here all the time. They just upgraded their menu. I got 2 proteins which are the shrimp and chicken katsu. So yummy.

name: Crif Dogs
address: 113 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10009, USA

reviews.author: Christopher Z
reviews.text: Stopped by and got a chili dog. The atmosphere was nice, there are old cocktail-style arcade games that can be used as tables. The dog was decent. And there's a bar hidden inside that people keep entering and leaving.

reviews.author: Maria R
reviews.text: Interesting options for different hotdogs. I got the one with corn,truffle fries and a slushy. The hotdogs was good the fries were eh not much truffle and the slushy wasnt that great.

reviews.author: Midori Yang
reviews.text:
Hot dog itself seems like it's deep fried so the skin is crisp while the inside stays juicy, with great pork flavor. The bun is pretty normal which seems like a waste when so much effort is put into the hot dog and toppings. Restaurant gets pretty loud and crowded with all the speakeasy customers but we were able to find a seat even on Saturday night.

The hot dogs are on the smaller side so you can have one as a light meal or combine it with tater tots. Splitting two hot dogs and a side of loaded tater tots between two people is good for a full meal.

Lil Ma hot dog - was intrigued by the peanut butter and it paired pretty well with the acidity of the pickles
Elote hot dog - not as impressed by this one but good if you like the toppings included
Nacho tater tots - reminds me of something you'd make at late night in a college dining hall and really hits the spot after a long day of walking around NYC

name: Yakitori Taisho
address: 9 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Nio Vezdeni
reviews.text:
I have never seen food of such poor quality in my life. We left everything untouched. It’s hard to believe that in the 21st century, in the middle of New York City, a place would serve food like this.

Where is the quality control? Where are the food standards? Why does this restaurant think people should pay for and eat food of this quality?

There is no other word to describe it—absolutely disgusting. This was the worst food I’ve ever had and the worst dining experience I’ve ever had. 🤢🤮

reviews.author: Alex S
reviews.text: Just came back here after 4 years! Supa tuna don still my favorite! But literally all the foods here is good. And have a great atmosphere for hang out and catch up with friends! 🤩👍

reviews.author: Roman Beylin
reviews.text: I really wanted to like this place, but the food was a major letdown. I came specifically for the yakitori, so I can’t speak to the rest of the menu, but what I ordered was disappointing. The chicken skin was soggy instead of crisp, the beef was tough and difficult to eat, and the garlic was served unpeeled. After a few bites, I left the meal mostly untouched. I get that the restaurant caters to a student crowd, but I was hoping for better quality. On the positive side, the atmosphere is nice and the staff is genuinely friendly.

name: Mountain House East Village
address: 23 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Alex Simmons
reviews.text:
Had an incredible dinner at Sichuan Mountain House! We started with the cold noodles, which were the perfect refreshing kick-off to the meal.
The highlights were definitely the Mala Prawns (loved the fun gloves they provided to keep things clean!) and the Mapo Tofu, which had the perfect balance of spice and numbness. We also ordered the eggplant and green beans, which were cooked to perfection.
A huge shoutout to our server, Emma. She was amazing. super friendly and gave us some fantastic recommendations that really made our night. We’ll definitely be back!

reviews.author: Saddmun Ahsan
reviews.text:
Great restaurant with large portions!

We visited on a Thursday evening and waited about 15 minutes for a table. One quick heads-up: the food here is authentically spicy, so definitely be prepared for that Sichuan heat!

We ordered a few different dishes to share. I personally didn't love the cold chicken, as it was a bit too sour for my taste. The Sichuan chicken, on the other hand, was easily my favorite! We also really enjoyed the peppercorn beef and the steamed fish. While the flavors were great, the fish was almost too spicy at times—even for someone used to some heat.

Overall, the portion sizes are very generous, which I really appreciated. It's a great spot as long as you can handle the spice!

reviews.author: Nicole Marengo
reviews.text:
I’ve been wanting to try Mountain House in the East Village for a long time since it’s often mentioned as one of the best Chinese restaurants in NYC. While the food was good and the portions were very generous, none of the dishes we tried stood out as significantly better than other Szechuan restaurants I’ve had in the city.
That said, the portion sizes are great for sharing, especially if you go with a group, and the staff was very kind and accommodating. The menu is also extensive and has a lot of interesting options.
The atmosphere was honestly the most underwhelming part for me. Online photos make the space look quaint but spacious, but in reality it’s quite small and can feel cramped. Luckily we sat in a booth, but I imagine the middle tables might feel a bit chaotic when it’s busy.
Overall, I still really enjoyed dining here and would definitely come back to try more dishes.

name: Mamoun's Falafel
address: 30 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Tom Elwood
reviews.text: Delicious, fast, cheap. Their signature falafels taste fresh and complex. All the flavors are rich and vibrant. Pita bread is very fresh. The interior decor is pretty minimal but comfortable enough.

reviews.author: vatsal shah
reviews.text:
One of the best bang for your buck eats out there in NYC.

The veg platter comes with 3 falafel balls so we added 2 adding to make it 9 balls total, got sauces on side and extra pita bread to make it a full meal for 2 people all under $20. The platter comes with enough hummus, baba ganoush, tahini and salad to make atleast 3 full pita sandwiches on your own.

Quick service, helpful staff that give you water when needed and more sauce if needed as well.

Downside? Food here doesn’t look pretty. That’s it. This is our personal favorite Mediterranean meal anywhere in the US when we want the best and freshest flavors without spending too much. Even the fancy looking Mediterranean spots can’t match the flavors here especially their falafels.

reviews.author: Vi Vi
reviews.text:
(6 MONTHS AGO)
Honestly wasn’t too happy today. Cuz what.

I ordered a falafel combo plate without pita. Now I’m wondering what if I tried the pita how would that even be?! 🫠

First off, not enough Tahineh sauce on my falafel!
2) The grape leaves were super mushy inside.
3) The feta cheese was weirdly too soft. & was missing the sprinkle of paprika. Oops.
4) The baba ganoush I know is supposed to be like squashed you know but it was like mushier than usual. Baba def don’t taste the same. (I can’t remember if there used to be a tiny drizzle of oil on top. Well, it was missing that I believe.)
5) The lettuce had all these brown spots on it & I ate one! Also cut too largely. Hard to shove in my mouth.
6) 6 tiny pieces of tomato on the plate.
7) Where the pickles, turnips, olives? I got NO OLIVES. 2 tiny slices of pickles, tasted weird. Tiny pieces of turnips, with a darker piece that might be bad, also tasted weird. & when I got the olives cuz at first, they ain’t give me no PTO so I had to ask for it. & even the olives tasted weird. Sorry.
Yeah the PTO honestly had a hint of fish & nail polish if I could describe it. It just tasted so weird I actually don’t understand why. Like it was bad. I’m 1,000% sure it’s old. & not being FIFO properly.
8) yo, where was the onions? You know what’s crazy? I used to say light onions. & now I’m looking for onions! This time I ordered it regularrrr with the onions & got 2 tiny pieces of onion?! Also, the onions are supposed to be covered in parsley. Where was the parsley? I’m sad.
9) where is the mint in the mint lemonade? I even swirled the drink before the photo.

The only thing that tasted good was the falafel & lentil soup. Rice was good too.

So I used to come here all the time. & this time was disappointing. I’m barely in the city & I decided to stop by beforee my firstt broadway show. I was so looking forward. To. Every. Bite.

However, during me watching a show, my stomach started rumbling & I thought it was someone else’s, but it was me unfortunately. My stomach became so gassy & I was letting a few loose here & there. So embarrassing. No one noticed thou.

I decided to eat most of the plate cuz I paid more than $20 for it. I left the bad pieces behind. & I was so looking forward to some good Mamoun’s like I just ate most of it. Like this is crazy. Before attending my first show too lol Pero like

& please why oh why is baklava the size of a QUARTER why. What ever happened to Mabrumeh & Knafe? Bring back the spinach pie! I know it’s so old. But so good.

Not the best counter vibes, low energy, not too welcoming.

I tipped on the kiosk when I ordered & even tipped cash.

I’m not okay right now.

The music needed to be louder.

& the streets smell like shit.

I can’t anymore.

Please make it right Mamoun’s.

TODAY (12/31/25)
CAME IN AGAIN TODAY FOR FALAFEL CRAVING. Also ordered grape leaves which were SO BAD.

Everytime I come here I always spend more than $20.

Here’s pics of my grape leaves that were hard & frozen on the inside. Soggy & mushy on the outside. I called St. Marks location to complain about it my grape leaves. They were so bad I spit them out. The manager was so rude & hung up the phone on me when I asked for a refund & told her quality of the food went down.

She was so rude that I cried. It’s been almost 2 hours & I’m still crying. This is not how you treat loyal customers. Very rude. Management needs to reconsider hiring a new manager bcuz I heard she’s very extremely rude to other customers too after some of my personal investigation. DO BETTER MAMOUN’S. It’s not how it used to be.

name: Cello's Pizzeria
address: 36 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Federico Pizzuto
reviews.text:
I put 5 starts to everything because for me this place is able to include all the best ingredients of New York City. Pizza is very very good… high quality and solid. I loved the Vodka Sauce and Pepperoni, the Burrata and the Chicory. But I would have tried more.
Service was quick and very pleasant. The guy served us with patience, answered all our questions and was nice.
The spot… it screams NYC and it is cozy with room to sit and enjoy the pizza (opposite to other fancier and more known places around). I loved staying there and I will definitely go back.

reviews.author: Christopher Gorrie
reviews.text:
I had a great quick bite at Cello’s Pizzeria while walking my way around Manhattan recently. The shop is low-key, but has a welcoming atmosphere, helpful staff, and really tasty slices.

I ordered a marinara slice and a pepperoni + jalapeño slice, along with a cold Peroni beer. The slices were crisp but not burnt, had zero flop, and the sauce was bursting with flavor. Toppings were good on the topping slice, too. Overall the meal was quick, warm, super tasty, and satiating.

I recommend checking this spot out if you’re hanging out in the East Village and are hankering for some great pizza. Or even if you just want a drink—they’ve got a pretty good selection to choose from.

reviews.author: Bryan Barber
reviews.text: Stopped in for a quick slice and asked the staff for a recommendation. They steered me to the burrata pizza. Good pick-me-up, I was happy! The clientele are a friendly bunch, just like the staff. This feels like a “happy place” where people hang out and socialize over pizza & beer.

name: Butter Smashburgers
address: 17 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Andy Liu
reviews.text:
Rating: 9/10
Date Visited: 1/16/2026

I ordered the House Smashburger Double ($11). I came here as part of a smashburger crawl and this was the best burger I had. The meat is cooked very well and goes great with the cheese, sauce, and additional toppings. The bun was toasted and buttered just enough to not take away from the burger itself but instead add to it. The price is pretty standard and a bit cheaper than some of the other places. Overall, I would recommend this place if you want a really good smashburger.

reviews.author: Jada Knight
reviews.text: Really loved the fries and dessert here, the cheeseburger was really good lots of flavor. Fig burger of month was okay a bit sweet for my taste, not a personal favorite but ill be back for a regular burger!

reviews.author: Raheem Ali
reviews.text: A really good Smashburger spot! Small menu which is good. The burgers were thin with lacy edges and full of flavour. The bun held well and the chips were also tasty. Nice clean interior. Would definitely recommend

name: Birria LES
address: 34 St Marks Pl #12, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Venuseudora
reviews.text: Top notch tacos with fast service! I went to this spot 2x within a week. I suggest the al pastor tacos chicken and beef are great. Generous filling and cooked to order. Maximum maybe 15 minutes wait time. It’s a small space inside to eat so can get busy but totally worth it. Their flan is yummy as well

reviews.author: Yu Jin In
reviews.text: my friend and i saw that Birria LES was at the holiday markets but the prices were insane so we came to their store locations instead (also they are all halal). there was no wait - we went on a saturday around 1pm, lots of tall chair counter / booth seating. the food was amazing. we kept going ordering more. the tacos were filling and the cilantro didn’t overpower, which i loved. the grilled cheese beef birria changes my brain chemistry!!!! i recommend everyone to try it, i was a little skeptical, but it was one of the most delicious and new experience i had all year. i would travel the hour from queens to eat the sandwich more often if i could. the nachos were yummy but it was hard to pull out without making a small mess. still would buy again. 12/10 food, experience, and space.

reviews.author: Zaineb Haider
reviews.text: i usually go to the birria LES on rivington st, but wanted to roam around st marks. i’m not sure if this is available at all locations, but the innovations on the menu are astonishing in the best way possible! they’ve got birria sliders (which my friend ordered,) birria dumplings, and birria texas toast garlic bread grilled cheese which is what i ordered. i got it with chicken- and no regrets. This was $15 but it kept me FULL. it’s a bit pricey (although not for new york,) but definitely worth it. never disappointed here.

name: The Bao
address: 13 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Ryan Li
reviews.text: The bao is the best authentic Chinese restaurant in the city. The atmosphere is very warming and comforting. The food is delicious and comes out fast. My server Misty is very knowledgeable about the food and always recommends the best food on the menu. Along with the great conversation with her she’s a great addition to The Bao. I wish I was able to give this place 10 stars but unfortunately I can only do 5 stars.

reviews.author: Iana Tchantchaleishvili
reviews.text: Reasonable price, delicious food and great atmosphere! I love St. Mark place and Asian restaurant choices there. I think the Bao is the good choice if you starving for Asian food that is also affordable. I one of my favorite there is steamed vegetable dumplings and I would recommend to taste it

reviews.author: Alec Ramirez
reviews.text: Very tasty and reasonably priced bao. The noodles I had definitely lacked flavor, but the pan fried pork soup ones were super tasty! The scallion pancakes were also quite good. I’d definitely come again

name: Kyuubi Sushi Omakase
address: 102 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10009, USA

reviews.author: Gabe Say
reviews.text:
Overall, we were somewhat satisfied with the meal, but it didn’t quite live up to expectations. We went on Valentine’s Day and paid $99 per person for the special menu (compared to their usual $68), so we were hoping for a more elevated experience.

The biggest drawback was the pace. It felt like one of the more rushed omakase experiences I’ve had, and not in a smooth or intentional way (all courses served in ~35 mins, with almost no explaining of the dish). Service was fine, but everything moved very quickly, which made it hard to fully enjoy each course.

The food itself was good, but not amazing. Some pieces were overly heavy on the wasabi, and there seemed to be inconsistencies in portioning. The sushi rice was slightly overcooked, and a few items like the ikura leaned too sweet for my taste. Small details like that made a difference, especially at this price point.

The music also didn’t really match the atmosphere, which added to the overall disconnect.

For what we paid, the experience felt underwhelming. Unfortunately, I wouldn’t recommend it, and I’m surprised by how high the reviews are given our experience.

reviews.author: Jessica Lin
reviews.text: Came here for my birthday in the East Village and had a great experience! Chef Ricky and our server Kiki were super warm and welcoming. The omakase fish was great value and high quality, the uni and wagyu was very fresh and they were generous with the caviar. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Nawh
reviews.text: Had a good omakase dinner on a saturday night. Had a group of 5 and they were very good to take care of us. Nigiri was on point. Unlimited sake for $18 was also a good deal .

name: Klong
address: 7 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Kan Chaichana
reviews.text:
This restaurant deserves five stars! My recent experience here was excellent, combining all the best qualities you look for when dining out.
First and foremost, the food was good. Every dish we ordered was fresh, flavorful, and perfectly prepared. It's clear that the kitchen takes pride in the quality of the ingredients and their execution.
Secondly, the price was good—in fact, it was great value for money. You often have to compromise on quality to save money, but here, the portions were generous and the quality was high without breaking the bank.
Finally, the service was good from the moment we walked in. The staff were attentive, friendly, and prompt. They made sure we had everything we needed without hovering, creating a genuinely enjoyable and relaxing atmosphere.

I highly recommend this spot and can't wait to go back!

reviews.author: Yami Tsao
reviews.text:
Tucked away on the artsy, character-filled stretch of St. Marks Place, this Thai spot absolutely blew me away. Truly one of the best Thai meals I’ve had in New York.

From the moment we walked in, the vibes were immaculate. The staff were so warm and welcoming — not just polite, but genuinely engaging. They walked us through the menu, chatted with us, and made the whole experience feel personal. The interior has such a cozy, atmospheric feel — warm lighting with subtle Southeast Asian touches that make the space feel intimate and special.

What we ordered:

🥤 Thai Iced Tea
Refreshing, perfectly sweet, and so good at cutting through the richness of the curries.

🍛 Panang Curry Chicken
Silky, rich, and deeply flavorful. The coconut and curry blend beautifully — my ultimate comfort dish.

🍜 Khao Soi (Curry Chicken Noodles)
So rich and satisfying. The curry-coconut broth is thick but not heavy, and the noodles soak up all that flavor perfectly.

🥩 Kor Moo Yang
Grilled pork neck that’s juicy but not greasy at all. Dipped in the sweet-savory sauce? So addictive.

🥩 Krapow Nuer
Solid, flavorful beef with authentic basil aroma, topped with a perfectly runny fried egg. So good over rice.

🍗 Wings & 🥗 Salad
Both packed with bold Thai flavors — that perfect balance of sour and spicy.

🧀 Crab Rangoon
Super cheesy, crispy on the outside, soft inside — comfort food done right.

✨ Favorite: Sao Nam
This one was magical. The layers of flavor are unreal — bright lime, creamy coconut, topped with pineapple and shredded ginger, shrimp inside, and a chewy, mochi-like wrapper. Sweet, tangy, fresh, aromatic — everything at once.

My friend and I had the best time. When we were leaving, the staff even refilled our Thai iced tea and packed it to-go for us to enjoy on the walk home. It felt so thoughtful — like we were truly taken care of.

I genuinely don’t want you to miss this spot.

reviews.author: Arushi Parikh-Kaushik
reviews.text: The curry had so much ginger, I think it cured the cold I would have in the future. My god. I love ginger and was feeling under the weather so I ate the whole thing though. All I'll say is, don't come on Saint Patrick's day. Service was great, the customers could've been better.

name: Veselka
address: 144 2nd Ave, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Berat Dijkstra
reviews.text:
Veselka has that warm East Village energy where it feels like people come at all hours just to reset for a bit. I ordered the pierogi plate and it came out simple but incredibly satisfying. The dumplings were soft with a slight chew and the fillings were comforting without being overly heavy.

I also tried the borscht which had a deep, slightly tangy flavor and came with a dollop of sour cream that rounded it out. A side of potato pancakes added crispness to the meal and worked well when paired with applesauce. Everything felt homemade in a way that doesn’t try too hard to impress.

The space itself feels lived-in and familiar, like it has seen every kind of New York day and just keeps going.

reviews.author: Kinga Surmiak
reviews.text:
We had the pleasure of visiting Veselka and were very positively surprised by this Ukrainian restaurant. On our first day, we ordered bigos and pierogi for takeout, and everything was absolutely delicious. The next day, we came back to try more dishes and enjoyed bigos again, chicken noodle soup, and Ukrainian borscht.
The portions are generous and definitely filling, the food is fresh, flavorful, and tastes truly homemade. Everything we tried was rich in flavor and very satisfying, especially after long days in the city.
If we ever visit New York again, Veselka will definitely be on our list of places to return to. This spot exceeded our expectations, and we highly recommend it to anyone looking for comforting, authentic Eastern European cuisine.

reviews.author: Brittany Marker
reviews.text: I had a great experience at Veselka. It is truly authentic. The proceeds from the beer I ordered (which was great) goes to help Ukraine, which is the reason I ordered it. My son and I were there for lunch and we each had matzoh ball soup (his first time having this, he enjoyed) and fried pierogis. I love that you can order all the varieties of pierogis without being pigeonholed to three of the same kind. Our server was very friendly and the atmosphere was very appropriately decorated. I’m glad we went.

name: Bungalow
address: 24 1st Ave, New York, NY 10009, USA

reviews.author: Pallavi Jain
reviews.text:
We recently visited Bungalow to celebrate our anniversary, and overall it was a lovely experience.

The ambiance is beautiful and perfect for a special occasion. We really enjoyed the vibe of the place—it feels elegant but still warm and welcoming.

For drinks, we tried the Guava Lemonade and a Falsa mocktail (I don’t remember the exact name), and both were refreshing and delicious.

For appetizers, we ordered the Dahi Kebab and Achari Stuffed Panner. Dahi Kebab was absolutely amazing—definitely one of our favorite dishes of the evening.

For the main course, we had the Okra dish and Old Delhi Chole. We really liked the okra—it was flavorful and well prepared. However, the chole was just okay and didn’t stand out as much as we expected.

For dessert, we tried the Rose Kulfi. If you are a big fan of rose flavor, you’ll probably enjoy it. For us, it was good for a one-time try.

One small detail that made the experience special was the little candy in a red packet at the end. It instantly reminded me of my childhood, which was such a sweet nostalgic touch.

Overall, it’s good to visit Bungalow at least once for the experience.

reviews.author: Prerana Dewan
reviews.text: hospitality, decor, and vibes are all a 10/10 here and vikas khanna is so warm and friendly. he really created such a beautiful restaurant and it shows in every detail. the food for me was hit or miss- the dishes were different and modern while incorporating traditional flavors which i appreciated, but i didn’t find the entrees to be extraordinary. the yogurt kebab was my favorite and the mango 3 ways was delish for dessert. would recommend to celebrate something here bc they bring a free dessert and he comes to your table to give it too which is so nice. worth experiencing once but its insane how hard it is to get a table here.

reviews.author: Ishi Sharma
reviews.text:
Visiting Bungalow has been a dream for us as big fans of Chef Vikas Khanna, and celebrating my partner’s birthday here was the perfect occasion. From the moment you walk in, you’re greeted by a beautiful, authentic aroma and homemade mitai in the lobby that sets the stage for an out of body experience.
We were completely starstruck by Chef Vikas; his humility and genuineness are incredible. He was working all night, personally engaging with guests, which added such a special touch to the evening.
The food is in a league of its own. The Yogurt Kebab was an absolute explosion of flavor, and the Chicken Anarkali blew my mind - the balance of tangy, cheesy, and spicy was perfection. We also loved the Pineapple Curry (it gets better with every bite!) and the Banana Ketchup was a delightful surprise. My only small note is that the Paneer Lazeez lacked a bit of the punch the other dishes had.
While the direct service was decent as our server wasn’t that warm and engaged, the head chef, hostesses and the server assistants were exceptionally kind and attentive. Truly a night to remember! Thank you Bungalow NYC team for the best night.

name: The Smith
address: 55 3rd Ave, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Cheryl Kosinski
reviews.text:
Excellent little gem in the east village.  I got the Mac n cheese and my son got the Smith burger with fries.  Both really tasty.  We had 4:45pm reservations and we seated immediately.  It was not crowded and was a nice ambient atmosphere.  We will definitely be back next time in that are of NYC!

Update February 2026
Went back again with my sisters.  Food was just as amazing and the service was great.  We had a reservation and were seated immediately.

reviews.author: Christine
reviews.text:
My best friend I visited The Smith for the first time yesterday and what a wonderful experience!! The restaurant is spacious, lovely, and CLEAN! Although it was fairly busy, we were seated immediately. The star of the show was our server CJ!! He was so kind and answered all the drinks + menu related questions we had. We were in between several items so we went with his suggestions for both drinks and apps & everything was DELISH!!

We will absolutely be returning & will specifically ask for CJ when we do!

reviews.author: Thiago Prado
reviews.text:
Truffle gnocchi was out of this world but very little amount. Legit needed to order 2 to have a normal size meal for a grown man. Lacked sufficient sauce but ordered more and after 10 minutes got it. I added chicken milanese to it but still wasnt enough.

Truffle fries also top notch.

Order the cookie or 9 layer cake for dessert.

name: La Palapa
address: 77 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Kevin
reviews.text: Food was so good, theyre mango Agua fresca was also really good. Had the chicken torta even though im usually not a fan of grilled chicken but this torta with the red sauce they have 🤌🤌🤌🤌. They also went above and beyond with service they told me they street corn was only on a cob and I said its fine but they brought it out on a bowl anyway. Owner and our server jose were really nice one of the best spots we went to on our trip. Good vibes

reviews.author: Shawn Hochhauser
reviews.text:
The experience here has been like no other. We were grateful to be let in without a reservation as a party of 5. We were sat quickly, and the drinks came even quicker.  We got happy hour frozen margs. Our favorites were hibiscus, passionfruit, and watermelon. The vibe inside was so authentic. The decorations were gorgeous; there is so much to look at and so many potential photos to take.

The meals were delicious and well portioned. So flavorful.

La Palapa is definitely a hidden gem. I will be returning to experience more of the menu and this amazing restaurant in total.

reviews.author: Homelandai
reviews.text: The restaurant was larger than it appears on the outside. Nice place, Cozy comfortable birthday celebration we had a great time. Marquita tuesday is a hit.

name: Nudibranch
address: 125 1st Ave, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Emily S
reviews.text:
If I could, I'd give 4.5 stars, and the only reason I'm holding back is because this place was great but we ordered a limited amount.

The interior is cute and comfortable and all the servers were attentive and knowledgable. We had a reservation and had no issues being seated immediately.

We started with the hamachi which was 10/10 delicious. Light and refreshing and a great start to the meal. I'll admit the chicken sandwich was bigger than we anticipated and could absolutely be a meal on its own. We split it, pretending it might be too large to finish but it was too good not to.

I will say the pork chop was the star of the show. This was so good that I watched my husband gnaw at the remains, not wanting to leave anything behind. The kimchi was nothing to phone home about but good, and the scallion pancake was absolutely a notch above the rest.

We didn't plan on getting dessert, but the masala chai tres leches looked too good to pass up and we left nothing behind. It was rich, cinnamon-y, and super satisfying in texture. I will be dreaming about that cake!

We agreed we would return at some point, and should we have a similar experience, I will bump this to 5 stars!

reviews.author: carlos borges
reviews.text:
Total Gem. From the moment you walk in, the staff makes you feel genuinely welcome. The atmosphere strikes a perfect balance—versatile enough for an intimate date, a solo dinner, or a lively group gathering. While he frog legs, soba noodles, and mushrooms were all expertly prepared, the beets in cashew ajo blanco were an absolute revelation.

The wine list is equally thoughtful; I tried a Chilean Orange Semillon that was completely eye-opening. I love the cohesive perspective here—the food, wine, service, and decor all feel intentional. I'm looking forward to trying other dishes on their menu. Highly recommended.

reviews.author: Cecilia Lee
reviews.text:
Discovered Nudibranch through the Red Book and honestly thought it was a bit mid. The portions were very small, though I assume that's intentional since most dishes are designed to be tapas style.

Mushrooms - 4/5
Enjoyed the cheesy mushroom flavor. Simple but satisfying

Chicken Sandwich - 4.25/5
Probably the standout dish. The chicken was big, juicy, and very tender. Highly recommend.

Hamachi - 3.75/5
Liked the tangy sauce pairing with the clementine

Uni Soba - 2.75/5
The uni tasted quite fishy and the soba itself was fairly bland, though the flakes added a nice bit of texture

Sea Bream - 3/5
A bit disappointing for an entree portion. I did appreciate that it was perfectly deboned, but the grilled top with added dill and pumpkin purée didn't pair that well IMO

Frog Legs - 2.75/5
Not usually something I order, but we saw a lot of tables getting it so we gave it a try. Unfortunately it had a noticeably fishy taste, would skip next time.

name: MáLà Project
address: 122 1st Ave, New York, NY 10009, USA

reviews.author: Valery Chen
reviews.text: OMG...the endless hunt for authentic mapo tofu is finally over. This is legit mapo tofu. We asked for extra spicy and they did not disappoint. So damn good!! Excellent with their purple rice. We also got their mala dry pot with beef tenderloin, lamb, wood ear, enoki, broccoli, and bean curd sheet, with their extra spicy base of 24 spices. This took me back to my study abroad in China. Absolutely 💯 amazing!!! Super flavorful, bold, and truly authentic. Everything was next level. Drinks were good and pair well with the spicy dishes. Service was friendly and attentive. The atmosphere is cute with their corrugated metal roof ceiling and the exposed brick floor. The haw flakes at the end threw me back to my 90s childhood! Will definitely be coming back for sure! What a treasure! Please don't change. It's hard to find such authentic, truly spicy, quality Sichuan cuisine!

reviews.author: Blake Rasmussen
reviews.text: A great Chinese restaurant offering tasty Sichuan cuisine in a relaxed setting. This is a fairly casual restaurant, but they serve some expertly mad dishes. The dry pot is their specialty and it’s deliciously spicy, fragrant, and mouth-numbing from Sichuan peppers - I strongly recommend you try the dry pot, but the marinated pickles and popcorn chicken were excellent too. They have a great happy hour special where food is discounted (our beer was full price), so take advantage of that before 7pm.

reviews.author: Alvin Domingo
reviews.text: Amazing food and Alexa is such a freaking great server! The duck salad, leftover fried rice and dried pot were so good! Great amount of spice but be careful of those peppercorns….WOOOOOOO!!! Lmao

name: Electric Burrito
address: 81 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: aaron grotas
reviews.text: Bare-bones burrito shop service was fast. I was able to order on the app. Loved my chicken California burrito worth coming to, but no real place to sit except for some basic stools. I wasn’t expecting a luxury sit down restaurant this was perfect for lunch.

reviews.author: Nelson
reviews.text: Great place for a burrito! Very small store with stools that face the wall, immediately walking in, what caught my eye was that the meat was super fresh! The guy in there was cutting the meat like it was a butcher and I just knew it would be good just from seeing that and knowing they don’t use frozen meat. I got the Hot Rod + my girlfriend got the Johnny, both were delicious. Coming from a person that eats a lot, the burritos here are huge! Left very full and they were very tasty, I loved the breakfast burrito but next time probably would just get the regular breakfast burrito no bacon, with the hot rod it felt like a lot was in it but it was still great

reviews.author: Behlul Ucar
reviews.text:
Good for a new york burrito. It was a little too dry, I got the conga burrito. They should make refried beans less dry, reduce the amount of rice and make sour cream more noticeable

I think it is one of the best burrito places in new York but does not compare to a california burrito.

name: The Office of Mr. Moto
address: 120A St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10009, USA

reviews.author: Molly
reviews.text: Heard great things about Mr Moto so we decided to try it — and it definitely lived up to the hype! Great food, fresh sushi, and such a fun atmosphere. Sushi chef Kenji was super friendly and made the experience even more memorable. Loved watching the sushi being prepared right in front of us. Will definitely be back!

reviews.author: Terence Fung
reviews.text:
Heard a lot about this place and finally had time to go try it. The whole experience is cute, and it's nice they created the lore around the whole place. The decor is also pretty nice, especially with the lounge and piano in the basement.

Unfortunately the food was only average. The whole dinner felt a bit rushed and basically for a 21 course omakase took only 1.5 hours. For other places it would normally take over 2 hours. Some pieces were quite good, but majority of the sushi was a bit bland and many pieces felt similar. The price itself was ok for the service and quality.

reviews.author: Yuliya Lubin
reviews.text:
Mr. Moto is an intimate, hidden gem that delivers simple, fresh, and elegant dishes. The experience begins before you even walk in — you need a secret entry code by calling the phone in the mailbox outside.

There are only 12 seats in total: 6 at the sushi counter and 6 at a private table, which makes the experience feel incredibly personal and exclusive. We were welcomed by the head chef from Japan, who has over 50 years of experience as a sushi chef. He presented a beautiful wooden box of fresh fish flown in from Japan that very morning.

Standout dishes included:• Bluefin tuna — incredibly rich and full of flavor• Beautifully presented appetizers• Torched scallop• Toro topped with Osetra caviar• A tender tuna hand roll to finish the meal perfectly

The intimacy of the setting, combined with smooth and attentive service, made the entire experience feel truly special. The bill at the end was also very reasonable compared to other omakase establishments in the city, especially considering the quality and freshness of the fish.

name: Craft + Carry Saint Marks
address: 116 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10009, USA

reviews.author: Vivianne Barretto
reviews.text: Very nice space and they have a backyard.  The choices for Craft Beers are very good.  The service is great and they have good Happy Hour prices.  I have to come back and have a beer in the backyard

reviews.author: Natasha
