reviews.text: Stopped here for a flight of beer before a show we were going to. Loved the selection, loved the location, and if we lived closer, I'd love to try one of each beer they have. Definitely a lot to choose from and not a bank breaker either. Would love to come back!

reviews.author: Don Ackad
reviews.text: Phenomenal selection - really cool guys working here too!  Great space and conversation. There’s even a fun bowling machine arcade game in the back. The guys were really knowledgeable and took the time to help me pick an IPA beer that I really enjoyed!  Will be back soon for sure!

name: Jazba
address: 207 2nd Ave, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Syed Ashrafulla
reviews.text:
Very busy restaurant serving up solid Indian food. I would not say it is spectacular but it can scratch a real itch for medium spice.

The mulethi was a nice classic fruit-forward tart gin drink with a cool presentation. The golgappa was fun (it's pani puri for fuschka to me) and I liked the mint chili the most, and this one had a cool presentation too. The murg makhani was decent and the biryani was OK but not as deeply flavored. I think taht's my main critique if I had one: the flavor depth was not as I'd expect, compared to Ishq or Kanyakumari. Still, it's a good place.

My dinner date did tell me that for the price, it's questionable whether to go back. I would agree, either drop the price or increase the flavor depth. Still, overall, I'd consider going back if asked by a friend.

reviews.author: Robin Picard
reviews.text:
If you’re looking for a dining experience that perfectly bridges traditional roots with a modern heartbeat, Jazba in the East Village is a must-visit. This Indian restaurant pays a sophisticated tribute to India’s legendary street food, making it an absolute standout for dinner.

To Sip
The craft cocktails are a masterclass in balance. I ordered the Mulethi, a gin sour riff with passion fruit and a sweet honeycomb tuile that highlighted the beautiful, earthy aroma of licorice root. My partner chose the Jamun, a vibrant mix of gin, Ume plum, and jamun-berry puree that was incredibly refreshing.

To Eat
The food is a love letter to Lucknow, featuring flavors that are hard to find elsewhere in NYC:

- Basket Chaat: A stunning potato basket filled with sweet potato, papdi, and chickpeas. It’s a crunchy, tangy explosion of flavor.

- Aminabad Ki Galouti Kebab: Inspired by the famous Tunday kebabs, these minced mutton kebabs were incredibly buttery and melted instantly. The accompanying warqi paratha was the perfect flaky pairing.

The menu also boasts impressive vegetarian options. The Atari Paneer featured tender house-made cheese in a rich tomato masala, while the Kashmiri Nadru Kofta, spiced lotus root dumplings in a velvety cashew sauce, was easily the highlight of our main course.

Jazba is a gem that manages to be both upscale and deeply soulful. Whether you're there for the innovative drinks or the regional specialties, it’s a journey worth taking.

reviews.author: Arpita Roddanavar
reviews.text: Went here as a group of five to celebrate a friend’s birthday, and we had a great time! The cocktail menu is wonderfully unique, with hints of Indian spices, they definitely hit the spot. For appetizers, we ordered several dishes and enjoyed all of them, but the Aminabad ke Galouti stood out with its tender, juicy meat. One item, the Basket Chaat, wasn’t quite right, but the staff kindly replaced it with an even better dish. For mains, the Railway Mutton Curry, Green Chilli Chicken, and Atari Paneer were flavorful, delicious, and were just the right amount of spicy. It’s also one of the rare places that serves Roomali Rotis, and we were so glad we ordered them! Overall, it was an amazing and memorable dining experience — just a tad on the expensive side, but well worth it!

name: Jiang's Kitchen 疆湖 - Halal Chinese Food
address: 65 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Hassan Ghaffar
reviews.text:
While exploring Manhattan, we stumbled across Jiang’s Kitchen and were pleasantly surprised to find it to be a halal restaurant. The restaurant has a cozy, no-frills atmosphere that lets the food do the talking, and it turned out to be one of those unexpected finds that makes a trip memorable.

We started with the House Special Lamb Kabobs, which arrived sizzling on skewers and coated with a flavorful blend of spices. The lamb was tender, juicy, and packed with smoky, cumin-forward flavors that kept us reaching for another bite. For appetizers, we ordered the Steamed Shumai, which were light, delicate, and filled with a savory, well-seasoned filling that paired perfectly with the dipping sauce.

The standout comfort dish of the meal was the Beef Wontons in Sour Soup. The rich, tangy broth had just the right amount of heat and depth, while the wontons were generously filled and incredibly satisfying. It was the perfect dish to balance the bold flavors of the lamb skewers.

Overall, Jiang’s Kitchen was an excellent surprise in Manhattan. From the flavorful lamb kabobs to the comforting wonton soup, every dish was delicious and full of authentic flavor. If you're looking for a halal-friendly spot serving satisfying Chinese cuisine with bold, spicy flavors, this hidden gem is definitely worth checking out.

reviews.author: NYCK SR
reviews.text:
This is hands down the best Chinese HALAL food I've had in Manhattan, especially in the East Village/Saint Marks. The dishes are not only delicious but also expertly crafted, the perfect blend of traditional Chinese flavors with halal ingredients. From the tender dumplings to the flavorful stir-fries, every bite is a delightful experience.
The restaurant's commitment to quality is evident, and the menu features a fantastic variety of options that cater to all tastes. The inviting atmosphere enhances the dining experience, making it a great spot for both casual meals and special occasions. If you're in the East Village, this place is a must-visit for anyone craving authentic and delicious Chinese halal cuisine.

reviews.author: Chloe
reviews.text: Disappointing experience. The prices and meat portions are good, but the food quality is lacking. The beef shumai felt like a frozen product, and the Big Plate Chicken was served with a very stingy portion of low-quality, non-hand-pulled noodles. The skewers were okay, but overall, it didn't live up to the reviews. I won’t be returning."

name: ChaHalo 茶话弄 - East Village
address: 23 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Glenn C
reviews.text:
Side Note: As per usual, I'm not a fruit tea guy, and I don't like sugary drinks, almost preferring things to be a bit bitter over sweet, so the 30% here is perfect for me.

I was at this location back in early December, shortly after they opened, with them having recently gone through their open promotions, I think the previous week. They have since opened another much more location in Chinatown on Mott, next door to Molly, and nearby a bunch of other more premium tea and bubble tea options. Anyway, this tea spot is from Xi'an in particular and has a few unique things, with one of the main pros for this location in particular being the design. Compared to some of the other tea places, it is very well designed, and it's a bit more calming in here, which makes this spot a good reprieve from the rest of the chaos on St. Marks.

In terms of the menu, they seem to specialize in more "floral" tea varieties such as Osmanthus Oolong. Their other signature seems to be Tea Frappés, which I haven't seen much of at Chinese tea shops. As I am a big fan of the former, I went with a medium Signature Osmanthus Oolong, at 30% sugar with no ice. The oolong was pretty solid with a pretty strong tea flavor, but not too bitter, and there was a good floral flavor from the osmanthus. It was worth the $6.99 +tax. It's better than any of the cheaper spots that focus less on tea leaves, and in comparison to the fancier spots, the only place comparable nearby is N25, and this is better.

This was a fancy but tasty oolong. It's a bit small for the price point, but I would stop by again.

reviews.author: Allen Zhang
reviews.text: The best roasted oolong in town. ChaHalo’s Rock Oolong Milk Tea has the perfect punch of bold, earthy tea flavor without being overpowered by the milk. It’s incredibly smooth and not overly sweet. If you love a strong tea-to-milk ratio, this is a must-try!

reviews.author: Raiwin M.
reviews.text: Loved this place! The Chinese tea here is wonderfully fragrant and so refreshing. Every sip feels smooth and calming — you can really taste the quality. ChaHalo has become one of my favorite tea spots! 🍵✨🥤

name: Dumpling Man
address: 100 St Marks Pl #7, New York, NY 10009, USA

reviews.author: Kyesha Turner
reviews.text: DO NOT EAT HERE. If I could give this place ZERO stars I would. The food is disgusting. $15 for 10 seared shrimp dumplings that were so hard & thick I couldn't eat them. They were all packed with corn & the shrimp was hard as a rock. I thought maybe if I boiled them in hot water I could soften them but no, still hard.  I have NEVER had dumplings this bad and I've lived in LES for 40 years. It was a teenage girl who cooked the food along with a couple in their 70s & 80s who need to retire. I got takeout or I would have asked for a refund. I still might.

reviews.author: Tom Barton
reviews.text: These were the worst dumplings I’ve had in New York, perhaps anywhere. Soup dumplings came out piled up and broken with no soup inside. Even the one or two that weren’t broken had no soup. How can you call these soup dumplings? I also ordered the seared shrimp dumplings. Mostly dough with one tiny shrimp and some corn inside. Who in their right mind is giving this place a high rating when there are such good places to get dumpings in the area? Who cares if they make them in front of you when they are bad dumplings that make you feel sick afterwards? I regret going here. Spend a few dollars more and get tasty dumplings at the dozens of places in the city that know west they’re doing, like Joe’s Soup Dumpings and Nan Xian.

reviews.author: Ming
reviews.text:
A solid spot to get dumplings in the East Village! Dumpling Man serves a variety of dumplings from savory to sweet and in fun, vibrant colors. They're made to order, so be prepared to wait a little bit. If you’re not sure what you want to get, there's a sampler option, where you can get two chicken, two pork, one shrimp, and one vegetable in a box. The dumplings here are stuffed, filling, hot, and fresh!

$9.55 steamed dumplings sampler

name: Cathédrale Restaurant
address: 112 E 11th St, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Sawako Y
reviews.text:
The Cathedrale is such a beautiful restaurant. The food, although pricey, was absolutely delicious, too. Depending on the occasion and seating available, I would definitely love to come back again.

We had a reservation but did have to wait a bit to be seated. There was a table in the restaurant but it seemed like there would be too much foot traffic, so we decided to sit at the bar, closest to the dining area.

The bar is huge and near reception were a bunch of people just standing around waiting for tables, which is why we’d chosen to sit closer to the restaurant. However, people still started hovering around behind us, so I’d choose a better seating option next time.

Although it has a romantic atmosphere, the noise level was quite high. So if you’re looking for a nice, quiet dinner, this probably wouldn’t be the place. It has a great vibe and is very lively, though.

And this is the place for warm, fresh bread and the best filet mignon.
The breadsticks were fine, but the mini bread loaf with butter was absolutely amazing.
I’m not sure if I’d order the oysters again, but the salmon dish was also delicious. (Faroe Island Salmon with panchetta, savoy cabbage, tarbaia beans and vin jaune)
My favorite, besides the bread, was the Filet Mignon with au poivre “Paul Bert” Paris mushroom duxelle. It was perfectly cooked, accompanied by a flavorful sauce.

I’ll be back for the delicious bread and filet mignon :)

reviews.author: silla dash
reviews.text:
We absolutely enjoyed our first experience at Cathédrale! The food, service, and ambiance were incredible. We’ll definitely be back.

Tao Group Hospitality is phenomenal, and they truly know how to create a memorable experience.

They made our anniversary dinner one to remember. ✨

reviews.author: Yuri Choi
reviews.text:
I was so happy to celebrate my friend’s birthday here. She came all the way from Louisiana, and this felt like the perfect place for it. The vibe is incredible. It feels elevated and beautiful without being overwhelming, and it really sets the tone for a special night.

The service stood out right away. The waiters and the sommelier were very informative and attentive, helping us choose a great bottle of wine and guiding us through the menu in a thoughtful way.

The sesame honey butter with the bread was honestly unforgettable. Such a small detail, but it really made an impression from the start. We had the tartare, prawns, octopus, and oysters, along with a bottle of wine, and everything was perfectly done.

What I appreciated the most was how they made sure my friend felt truly celebrated. They were so sweet about the birthday, with a card, cake, and beautiful presentation that made the whole experience feel even more special.

Definitely a place I would come back to for another meaningful occasion.

name: Vegan Grill
address: 58 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Patty Barrientos
reviews.text:
I visited yesterday and order Vegan Gluten free Pancakes 🥞 with hash browns and mushrooms, love the pancakes!, the mushrooms flavor, the hash browns were ok, it didn’t feel/taste like homemade, but still aceptable

The thing I love the most was the service!, such a nice men took my order and gave me the GF options, with so much patience and very friendly!!! ☺️

They have a lot of options on their menu, for me as a vegan/gluten free/soy free person, it’s always so hard to find some items such as pancakes and waffles (I wish they have waffles available soon), so I’ll definitely will be back!!!

reviews.author: Stephanie Salas
reviews.text:
Amazing vegan breakfastat!
We got the pancake platter and the breakfast burrito, both were delicious!

I especially loved the pancakes. I got the regular ones and they were just perfect.

reviews.author: V M
reviews.text:
Nice cozy spot for food.

Went here randomly because it was the closest vegan restaurant in the area

I really enjoyed it , I love that they have options like beans and jackfruit , I really get bummed with its only impossible products..

I would definitely go back

name: Osakana East Village
address: 42 1/2 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Tyler Rieger
reviews.text: We took the Uni Shucking class. Our instructor was Corey and he was so awesome. Very helpful, friendly, and made the experience so memorable. He walked us through how to shuck and was there every step of the way to help us if we needed. While we were busy shucking, Corey got to know us a bit more and was able to ask us about our favorite fish to each, which he later cut up for us and included in our hand roll extravaganza! The portions were insane, we had so many leftovers to keep us happy for a few days extra. Make show to show up hungry! Would highly recommend this class to anyone looking for a fun, UNIque experience.

reviews.author: Elain c
reviews.text: Ordered the nigiri standard- waited for about 10minutes. I’ve been on a mission to find the best sushi spot in the city. Probably one of the best sushi spots I’ve had so far that is privately owned and not a chain. The fish melts in your mouth and the sushi rice is seasoned. Pieces are nice and fat. You’re paying for high quality fish and the prices do reflect its standards. Osakana, you’ll be seeing me soon ;)

reviews.author: Brian Yuan
reviews.text: I don't normally write bad reviews but this place essentially stole our money. Long story short: during COVID times, my wife and I bought $360 worth of gift cards from their website since eating in was the thing back then - the gift cards were mailed to us. Life got busy and we forgot about the gift cards. Fast forward to last week, I tried using them in store but the cashier said they had $0 balance and were cashed out a few years ago in October 2023. Hard to believe and unable to give them the benefit of the doubt seeing how we don't live with anyone else and we've never set foot in the store until last week. Do not buy any gift cards from them.

name: Benemon
address: 108 E 4th St, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Maria R
reviews.text: Not the best service we had to wave down the server to take our order she was just chatting at the bar then the table thay was sat after us got there food before us app and main before we even got our food. The drinks were good those came out fast and we're delicious. The food was alright not that great for the pricing and portions small.

reviews.author: Mei Deng
reviews.text:
FIREEE love this place. Fav place for unagi don. Prices are so good for the quality

- Unagi don: 11/10, best unagi don, so tender, and everything is really flavorful. The egg was yummy too
- Tuna tataki: 9/10, usually not the biggest fan of tuna or sashimi-like dishes, but this was yummy

reviews.author: Josephine Lee
reviews.text:
Major dining pet peeve: Restaurants that are stingy with AC on hot summer days. Come on, we all have electric bills to pay, and we all know how high they get from May to Sept. But no one wants to sweat like they're doing HIIT while trying to enjoy a plate of hot food. Oooo, weak pee in my gnocchi....tasty.

Benemon is another stingy-with-AC restaurant. On a sweltering day with 251,000% humidity, the air conditioner was barely on, the fan was non-existent, and perhaps most insulting of all, they served us tepid, room temperature water. We had to ask for ice, which they (begrudgingly) gave in small cubes that melted instantly.

The food was fine, but not worth the suffering.

We started with Beef Carpaccio that was a bit too cooked for my liking, and Tuna Tataki likely drowned a painful death in the too-sweet miso-nut sauce. The Soft Shell Crab was pretty good, but I could've used more than six tiny legs. My Katsu Curry featured cute, sausage-shaped  cutlets, but most of the dish was just sticky rice and gravy that I could've made myself.

In attempt to avoid feeling feverish, I also got Yuzu Shiso Spritzer to wash everything down. It was fine, but if I hadn't desperately needed more ice than the staff wanted to give, I wouldn't have bothered ordering this.

Not worth the sweat or the schlep.

name: Xi'an Famous Foods
address: 129 2nd Ave, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Suzy
reviews.text: Congrats to another location! It’s re-opened in St. Marks Pl on 2nd Ave, close to their other location on 4th Ave. They have 2 tables that fit 2ppl and 3ppl. It’s small, but enough to grab a quick meal. Staff are nice. The lamb noodles are so yummy. I got it with regular spice, but you can adjust the level for less. The spicy and a bit numbing flavor is so good. Their dumplings are so good also. The chrysanthemum tea goes well with the meal. I went during the grand opening promo. So yummy. Thanks! The more I eat it, the more I like it.

reviews.author: Nathan LIU
reviews.text: New location. Still same great quality. Really nice spot for quick meal! If you love spicy, come here!

reviews.author: Jacob R
reviews.text: The N1 cumin lamb noodles were decent, but the spice level was overkill. It totally masked the cumin, which was a bummer because that's what I was going for. I wish the heat was dialed back so the actual flavors could pop. Good noodles, just way too much chili.

name: Moko
address: 138 2nd Ave, New York, NY 10003, USA

reviews.author: Taku Shimura
reviews.text: It was my first time going here. I've always wanted to try omakase, too. The place is comfortably small and quaint. The service is very friendly and attentive. The sushi quality is superb! The flavoring is quite tasty, too, although I do prefer more unadulterated pure sashimi fish pieces to speak for their own natural flavor. I'd still go back for their superb sashimi quality. Only negative is that I wish the chef spoke better English or even Japanese. I had trouble understanding what each omakase serving was...

reviews.author: Ian Orr
reviews.text: My favorite Omakase! Great location in East Village and the team is simply wonderful from Daniella and Veera to Chef June, everyone there feels like family. Service is quick and you leave satisfied for such an affordable price and a fabulous omakase experience. The fish is so fresh that it feels like the first time every time.

reviews.author: Jessica Choe
reviews.text:
(G)o for girls night
(W)owed by the attentive service (but management is very slow to reply)
(A)mbiance was intimate and for pairs or couples
(M)enu was limited but that's to be expected for omakase, portion sizes are very small.
Best menu item was the uni shooter. Also flagging no drinks only mocktails.

name: Sushi Mumi
address: 130 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10009, USA

reviews.author: Daniel Chen
reviews.text: A wonderful omakase experience at Sushi Mumi. The owner/chef Marco definitely showcased the best of the Japanese culinary skills and selected some of the most fresh and delicious ingredients available during the season. He was also a friendly and fun person to chat with. Food wise, each course is prepared with care and detail, well presented and tasted remarkable. My favorite was the hairy crab, which I ordered 2 additionals. The sake selection, although bot vast, but had some interesting choices. We tried Hitakami日高见 brand sake; it tasted veey good. The service staff was also friendly and courteous. I highly recommended this restaurant to anoyone yearning for fine sushi dining.

reviews.author: esther
reviews.text:
I had an amazing omakase experience here. Every course was beautifully prepared and incredibly delicious. You can tell how much care and attention Chef Marco puts into every detail—from the appetizers and fish to the presentation and pacing of the meal. Each dish was perfectly balanced and memorable.

Without a doubt, this was one of my top omakase experiences. I would highly recommend it to anyone looking for a truly special sushi experience. I can’t wait to come back.

reviews.author: Patrick Wong
reviews.text:
TLDR: Sushi Mumi is a proper omakase in the East Village where Kura used to be. Chef is a former Ginza Onodera alum so if you're missing that kinda flavor since they've closed - this is a good spot to pick.

Came to Sushi Mumi after a few folks recommended it. It occupies the former space where Kura was (RIP) and a former Ginza Onodera chef runs the counter.

The menu is on the higher tier of the sushi circuit at $250 before tax/gratuity with 5 otsumami, 10 nigiri/sushi, tamago, soup, dessert. The ostumami definitely allowed chef to show off his skills with the cooked dishes.

The the hamo or pike conger which was served with jasmine vinegar jelly was the crowed favorite. Very tender with that fragrant scent of the jasmine and the tang of the vinegar jelly. The amadai fried with the scales on was also enjoyable mixed with the hairy crab sauce. The grilled nodoguro was excellent as well.

The shari is cooked very well as you'd expect from someone of this caliber. There's a deep red hue with the nice salty tang from the vinegar. The nigiri was excellent with the stand outs for me being the shima aji and iwashi. It's a comfortable restaurant and if you're looking for a break from the budget omakases - this is a spot that a lot of sushi connoisseursm frequent.
